requirement: Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Supplier information is accurately captured and stored in the system.
Supplier data is up-to-date and reflects the current status of the supplier.
Supplier performance is tracked and evaluated based on predefined criteria.
Buyer can approve or reject purchase orders based on predefined approval rules.
Purchase orders are automatically generated and sent to suppliers for approval.
Supplier information is accessible to authorized users for procurement purposes.
Supplier performance data is used to make informed procurement decisions.
Carsome can manage suppliers centrally, with standardization and visibility into organizational spend.
Carsome can access real-time data on suppliers, requisitions, orders, invoices, and payments.
Coupa is integrated with Carsome's existing ERP system (NetSuite) for critical master data synchronization.
Carsome has a comprehensive change management plan in place to drive adoption of new supplier processes and the Coupa platform.
Data migration from legacy systems to Coupa is planned and executed as a separate workstream.
Carsome's procurement and Supplier Management policies are reviewed and updated to align with the capabilities of the Coupa system.
Carsome's technology infrastructure is prepared to support the cloud-based Coupa solution.
This document is a key input for the overall project governance framework, guiding scope management, progress tracking, and decision-making throughout the implementation lifecycle.
Buyer can initiate the new supplier registration process by providing basic information.
Supplier information is accurate and current, and all suppliers are vetted through proper approval channels.
Supplier data is maintained accurately and visibility to certifications and contact information is ensured.
Supplier onboarding process is streamlined, and multiple channels of managing information are eliminated.
Supplier participation is encouraged, and accurate supplier data is maintained.
Compliance and procurement policies are enforced, and maverick spending and non-compliance are prevented.
Buyer can create a new supplier request with required details.
System checks for potential duplicates and highlights missing or incomplete fields before submission.
Approval process is initiated automatically after submission of the new supplier request form.
Approvers can provide rejection comments, which are received by the requester for review and resubmission.
Coupa auto-triggers the External Onboarding Form after approval of the new supplier request form.
Supplier can complete the External Onboarding Form via email or by registering on the Coupa Supplier Portal.
Supplier can fill in additional details required for successful onboarding.
Buyer can create a supplier profile with required details such as company name, address, contact information, and bank details.
Buyer can classify a supplier as local, foreign, or individual and capture relevant details accordingly.
Buyer can conduct a risk assessment for a supplier and capture relevant details such as primary commodity, other commodity, and risk assessment questionnaire.
Buyer can obtain vendor acknowledgment for supplier's anti-bribery and corruption policy and purchasing terms and conditions.
System can store and manage various documents required for supplier onboarding such as tax certificate, statutory document, and non-disclosure agreement.
Buyer can configure payment types and bank details for a supplier.
Supplier can be onboarded with required details such as company name, display name, primary contact, primary address, and vendor type.
Supplier can provide bank details such as payment types, bank form, and payee email address.
Supplier can complete risk assessment questionnaire and provide risk assessment completed sheet.
Supplier can acknowledge and agree to anti-bribery and corruption policy, general terms and conditions of purchase, and other mandatory documents.
System can classify vendors based on their type and provide relevant documents for verification.
Buyer can create a supplier profile with required details such as vendor type, classification, company name, primary contact, address, and payment information.
Buyer can conduct a risk assessment for a supplier and capture relevant details such as primary commodity and other commodity.
Supplier can acknowledge and agree to the company's anti-bribery and corruption policy, general terms and conditions of purchase, and other relevant policies.
System can store and manage relevant documents for a supplier such as tax certificates, statutory documents, and non-disclosure agreements.
Supplier can submit their onboarding form with required documents.
Vendor Management Team validates supplier data and checks ABC link details.
Vendor Management Team reviews and approves NDA, or rejects with comments and requests signed version.
Risk Manager reviews Risk Assessment information for High-Risk commodities and approves or rejects with comments.
Finance Vendor Team reviews supplier financial data and updates internal finance information.
Supplier can receive purchase order via email or manual send.
Invoice matching level can be set to 2-way or 3-way.
The system allows the Business or Procurement team to initiate the internal supplier registration process by providing basic information.
The system facilitates an internal approval workflow for the supplier registration request.
After approval, the system sends the registration request to the requester, prompting them to register the supplier and complete due diligence steps.
The system integrates supplier details from Coupa Supplier Information Management (SIM) into NetSuite, creating a new supplier record.
The system synchronizes key details from NetSuite with the corresponding supplier record in Coupa.
The system updates the supplier's status in Coupa to 'Active' after successful integration, indicating that the supplier is fully onboarded and eligible for use.
The Tax Team reviews and validates tax information provided by the supplier, updating the form accordingly if Withholding Tax (WHT) applies.
The Tax Team submits and approves the tax form after verification is complete.
The Head of Procurement verifies the submitted details and approves the form after completing their validation.
The Head of Procurement rejects the form with comments and clarification if any changes are required, sending it back to the supplier for corrections.
System performs a duplicate check on supplier details and stops the onboarding process if the supplier is not valid.
Approved suppliers can be onboarded through the Internal Supplier Registration Form.
Requester can complete the Internal Supplier Registration Form with detailed supplier information and submit it for further approvals.
Approvers can review and approve or reject the supplier request with comments, and the Requester can resubmit the request with necessary corrections.
Supplier details can be updated and saved in the system.
System can generate and send purchase orders to approved suppliers.
Buyer can create a supplier profile with company details and profile information.
Buyer can add bank details for a supplier, including payment types and bank forms.
Buyer can conduct a risk assessment for a supplier, including primary and other commodities.
Supplier must acknowledge and agree to the company's anti-bribery and corruption policy and general terms and conditions of purchase.
Supplier must provide required documents based on their classification, including business identification documents and tax certificates.
Buyer can create a supplier profile with required details.
Buyer can classify a supplier as local, foreign, individual, or government.
Buyer can request and manage non-disclosure agreements (NDAs) from suppliers.
Buyer can view and manage supplier bank details, including payment types and bank forms.
Buyer can conduct risk assessments on suppliers and view completed risk assessment sheets.
Buyer can obtain acknowledgments from suppliers for anti-bribery and corruption policies, general terms and conditions of purchase, and other agreements.
Buyer can manage and store supplier documents, including business licenses, certificates of incorporation, and tax certificates.
Buyer can create a supplier profile with required details such as vendor type, classification, name, address, and bank details.
Buyer can upload required documents for supplier verification such as certificate of incorporation, tax residence, and identity card.
Buyer can conduct risk assessment for suppliers and capture primary and other commodities, risk assessment questionnaire, and completed sheet.
Buyer can obtain vendor acknowledgement for anti-bribery and corruption policy, general terms and conditions of purchase, and unlawful activities act.
Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers with required details such as supplier name, address, and payment terms.
Requester can submit the Internal Supplier Onboarding form with required details.
Vendor Management Team reviews the submitted form for accuracy and completeness, and rejects or requests corrections if necessary.
Vendor Management Team performs supplier data due diligence, including checking ABC link details, and updates the internal onboarding form accordingly.
Risk Manager reviews the Risk Assessment information for High-Risk commodities and approves or rejects the form accordingly.
Finance Vendor Team reviews the submitted details with a focus on supplier financial data and updates the relevant internal finance information.
Head of Procurement reviews the form for Foreign Vendors and approves or rejects it accordingly.
Tax Team reviews and validates tax information provided by the supplier, and updates the form accordingly.
Head of Procurement verifies and approves the submitted details, or rejects the form with comments and clarification.
Supplier record is created in Coupa upon approval, and in NetSuite upon successful integration.
Supplier details are seamlessly integrated from Coupa SIM to NetSuite, and vice versa.
Supplier status in Coupa is updated to 'Active' upon successful integration from NetSuite.
Requester or Procurement team initiates the vendor update process, and Supplier proactively updates the supplier Onboarding form.
Supplier Onboarding form is updated with new details, including Company Details and Profile, and additional fields such as Country of Onboarding.

Approved supplier records are successfully updated in NetSuite.
Rejected supplier records are sent back with comments for correction and may be resubmitted after necessary updates.
Supplier details are seamlessly integrated from Coupa SIM to NetSuite to update a Supplier record in NetSuite.
Key details are automatically synchronized from NetSuite to Coupa to update the Supplier in Coupa.
Buyer can initiate the update process for internal existing vendors.
Buyer can complete the vendor update form with updated vendor information and submit it for internal review.
Buyer can provide required vendor information, including company details and profile, bank details, risk assessment, and vendor acknowledgment.
System displays conditional document sections based on vendor classification.

System can prevent approval of supplier form unless required details are provided.

Head of Procurement verifies and approves submitted details.

Rejected comments are notified to the requester and the requester can resubmit the request with corrected information.

Supplier details are automatically synchronized with the corresponding Supplier record in Coupa after update in NetSuite.
Procurement Team or Finance Team can update vendor status.
Update may include actions like placing a purchase block, extension of Entity, deactivation and reactivation of a supplier in the system.
Update request is routed to the Vendor Management Team, Finance Team and Head of Procurement for Approval.
Supplier details will be seamlessly integrated from Coupa Supplier Information Management (SIM) into NetSuite.
Supplier records will be updated in NetSuite ensuring the vendor record reflects the current and approved status.
One-time vendor transactions in Coupa will be created using a dummy or sundry account configured in the system.
Supplier records will be approved by Procurement/Risk Manager/Tax Team/Head of Procurement.
Supplier records will be created in NetSuite by Procurement.
Supplier status will be updated in Coupa based on the status labels (Active, Onboarding, Inactive).
Supplier portal status will be updated in Coupa based on the status labels (Invited, Draft/Inactive, Name and Email Missing, Linked, Not Linked, Name Missing).
Supplier details, including ABC link, are captured during the supplier onboarding process.
One-time vendor transactions are created under a dummy/sundry vendor.
